patterns_found_description: Unit of Work via DbContext; Simple Factory in ProductsController
solid_notes: - Controllers depend directly on EF DbContext and entities (no Repository/Service abstractions), reducing dependency inversion. | - Business rules live in controllers (transaction scripts), so SRP and Open/Closed are weak; changes to workflows require controller edits. | - Entities are anemic data carriers; behavior is not co-located with data, risking violations of encapsulation. | - Some positives: DbContext scopes transactions/unit-of-work; optimistic concurrency token on products; clear aggregate boundaries in entities.
